task: Built robust multi-session concurrent architecture with file locking, session registry, and heartbeat lifecycle management; fixed extension duplicate-spawn bug, removed plaintext secrets from git history, wrapped task queue operations in flock to prevent race conditions, verified heartbeat/stale-session detection, implemented atomic stats writes with per-session statusline caching and race-safe daemon cleanup, refactored daemon to spawn companion sessions instead of killing live user sessions, and fixed abrupt-jump UX by waiting for current turn to finish before opening companion terminal.
discussion_summary: The project has evolved from basic multi-session support to a production-hardened concurrent system with graceful session lifecycle management and user-centric UX. Prior sessions established flock-based file locking, session registry infrastructure, fixed the extension context-notification duplicate-Claude bug, removed secrets from git history, wrapped queue operations in file_lock, and refactored the daemon to spawn companion sessions instead of killing user sessions. This session identified and fixed a critical UX flaw: the daemon was opening a companion terminal immediately upon trigger, interrupting the user's current turn mid-flow. The fix implements turn-aware waiting in lifecycle.py—the daemon now waits for the current turn to finish before spawning the companion, preserving user context and terminal focus. All changes committed and pushed; daemon re-enabled for live monitoring.

accomplishments.what: Verified multi-session file locking implementation (e2f7cb5) with flock and session registry infrastructure
Confirmed session-scoped edit cursor prevents parallel session file corruption (commit 3ef2974)
Validated multi-PID registry daemon kills all parallel sessions cleanly (commit 320e652)
Reviewed project-state handover mechanism for parallel session composition (commit 8e01e8a)
Diagnosed duplicate Claude instance bug: extension.js createTerminal called unconditionally on context/goal_launch notifications
Added session-active guard checks to extension.js context notification handler (line 189) and goal_launch handler to prevent re-spawning
Discovered plaintext webhook secret in askr_state/config.json committed to git despite gitignore rules
Removed config.json from git history via git rm --cached and verified gitignore is enforced
Committed security fix (7735e19) without Claude as co-author; verified all untracked files committed and pushed cleanly
Identified task queue race condition: session_start drain could silently wipe tasks appended by CLI during concurrent execution
Wrapped all queue file operations in file_lock: CLI queue write (askr/cli/askr.py), session_start drain (askr/hooks/session_start.py), checkpoint auto-queue (askr/session/checkpoint.py)
Verified file_lock implementation and tested lock-wrapped queue operations end-to-end with manual sanity tests
Committed task queue race fix (c79fe13) with message 'fix(tasks): lock queue file across read/write to prevent silent task loss'
Verified heartbeat logic task: confirmed register_session, update_heartbeat, _is_alive, and get_active_sessions already fully implemented
Refactored daemon lifecycle to spawn companion sessions instead of killing live user sessions (commit 7a11a12)
Implemented per-session statusline caching and race-safe daemon cleanup with cooldown state machine (commit 2d39e5d)
Fixed abrupt-jump UX by implementing turn-aware waiting in lifecycle.py—daemon now waits for current exchange to finish before opening companion terminal (commit f258587)
Documented daemon spawn refactor decision: companion session preserves user context and prevents terminal disruption
Documented turn-aware waiting decision: prevents abrupt interruption of user's mid-flow interaction
Documented removal of idle-wait loop: turn-aware waiting is more deterministic and user-centric

next_actions.action: Monitor daemon logs for 24h+ to verify companion spawn stability and turn-aware waiting behavior in production
next_actions.why: Critical validation that the turn-aware fix resolves UX disruption without introducing new race conditions or timing issues

next_actions.action: Test askr viability with concurrent user + monitor sessions: run user session while daemon spawns companion, verify both sessions remain stable and user context is preserved
next_actions.why: End-to-end validation that the refactored daemon lifecycle works correctly in real concurrent scenarios

next_actions.action: Commit pending state files: git add askr_state/* && git commit -m 'docs: update decisions, goals, and failed approaches for turn-aware companion spawn'
next_actions.why: Persist session decisions and goals to git for continuity across future sessions

decisions.decision: Daemon spawns companion session in new terminal instead of killing user's live session
decisions.reason: Preserves user context, prevents terminal disruption, and enables true concurrent background task execution without losing interactive session state

decisions.decision: Companion spawn waits for current turn to finish before opening new terminal
decisions.reason: Prevents abrupt interruption of user's mid-flow interaction; respects user's active turn before introducing new terminal

decisions.decision: Removed idle-wait loop that was causing abrupt jumps
decisions.reason: Idle-wait was creating unpredictable timing; turn-aware waiting is more deterministic and user-centric

failed_approaches.approach: Killing the user's live Claude session to spawn a new one for background tasks
failed_approaches.reason: Caused context loss, terminal disruption, and poor UX; users lost their active conversation state when daemon needed to run background work

failed_approaches.approach: Immediate companion spawn on trigger without waiting for turn to finish
failed_approaches.reason: Caused abrupt UX disruption by opening new terminal mid-flow, interrupting user's current interaction

relational_files.why: Daemon monitor calls lifecycle.spawn_companion_session with turn-aware waiting
Extension spawns initial Claude session; daemon companion spawn is separate flow
Lifecycle uses registry to track active sessions and determine turn state
